events.label: assistant
events.text: I’ll create the required JSON file directly, treating the omitted cotter pin as a failed mechanical retention condition on a steering/suspension fastener.

events.label: shell
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc "cat > /app/answer.json <<'EOF'
{
  \"decision\": \"fail\",
  \"risk\": \"critical\",
  \"work_stage\": \"rough-complete\",

  \"percent_complete\": 80,

  \"order\": [],
  \"workable\": [],
  \"findings\": [
    \"Cotter pin is omitted from the castle nut, leaving the joint without required positive mechanical retention.\",
    \"Castle nut can loosen under vibration or steering/suspension loads, creating risk of joint separation and loss of vehicle control.\"
  ],
  \"actions\": [
    \"Stop progression past the quality gate until corrected.\",
    \"Install the correct OEM-specified cotter pin after verifying castle nut torque and slot alignment.\",
    \"Reinspect the affected suspension or steering joint and document acceptance before closeout.\"
  ],
  \"rationale\": \"A castle nut used on a suspension or steering joint requires the cotter pin or equivalent OEM-specified locking device to provide positive retention. The observed value is zero cotter pins installed where one is required, so the assembly is out of tolerance and unsafe for release.\",
  \"references\": [\"OEM service procedure\", \"OEM torque and fastener-retention specification\", \"OEM alignment specs\"]
}
EOF
